info About the Festival

Why CCTF?

关于芝加哥中文戏剧节

Chicago has never lacked people who love theatre, nor creative practitioners working in Chinese-language theatre. CCTF is the platform they were missing.

The story behind the festival

Over the years, different schools and communities have continued to develop their own productions and ensembles — some rooted in campuses, others deeply engaged in community spaces; some focused on classical texts, others exploring contemporary forms of expression.

Yet these efforts have often remained disconnected from one another, and audiences have usually encountered Chinese-language theatre only within small familiar circles.

The Chicago Chinese Theatre Festival hopes to create a more open, collaborative platform: one that connects creative communities across locations, creates opportunities for new directors, actors, playwrights, and audiences to encounter one another, and makes theatre not only a matter of performance, but also a gateway to learning, exchange, and collaboration.

另一个重要的出发点是语言的可及性。在芝加哥，有不少华人观众并非不愿走进剧场，而是缺少可以用母语观看与理解的戏剧内容。我们希望芝加哥中文戏剧节能提供这样一个场所：让观众可以用中文观看、理解与感受戏剧，并在其中共享经验与情感。
link

Connecting Campus & Community

CCTF bridges UChicago, Northwestern, SAIC, and community groups that had previously worked in isolation — placing their work on the same map for the first time.

school

A Platform for Learning

Beyond performances, CCTF offers free workshops, masterclasses, and industry talks — building real skills and real connections in the Chicago theatre ecosystem.

translate

Linguistic Accessibility

Chinese-speaking audiences deserve theatre in their own language. CCTF creates that space — without asking anyone to translate their feelings to experience art.

4
Theatre Groups
戏剧团体
5
Productions
戏剧作品
13
Performances
场演出
7
Workshops & Talks
课程与对谈

The Four Groups

四个联合发起团体

University of Chicago Windmill Chinese Drama Club
芝加哥大学风车剧社

One of the oldest and most influential Chinese-language theatre organizations at UChicago. Since its founding, Windmill has continued to build stages between campus and city, using Chinese-language theatre to connect Chinese-speaking students, young creators, and local audiences. It is the primary initiator and main venue host of CCTF 2026.

Productions in CCTF 2026

A Doll's House | 《玩偶之家》 · May 17–19 arrow_forward No Exit | 《禁闭》 · May 22–24 arrow_forward The Flying Heart | 《会飞的心》 · May 20 arrow_forward
Dong Fang Drama Group
东方话剧团

Unlike a campus-based student group, Dong Fang Drama Group is a long-standing community theatre troupe rooted in Chicago. Founded in 2005, its members — coming from all walks of life in the local community — have remained committed to theatrical creation and performance, staging dozens of Chinese and international classics as well as original works over twenty years.

Productions in CCTF 2026

An Act of Grace | 《杀了我》 · May 31 arrow_forward
Northwestern University Eighthday Theatre Club
西北大学周八剧社

Founded in 2021, Eighthday Theatre Club is Northwestern University's first Chinese-language theatre troupe. Its name comes from "the eighth day" — an imaginary day between Sunday and Monday — symbolizing a space of imagination where Chinese-speaking students living abroad can express themselves freely and find release.

Productions in CCTF 2026

Hidden Treasure | 《隐藏的宝藏》 · May 29–30 arrow_forward
SAIC Chinese Theatre Club
芝加哥艺术学院中文剧社

A young and energetic creative group, SAIC Chinese Theatre Club brings together students and practitioners from diverse artistic backgrounds at the School of the Art Institute of Chicago. Compared with more traditional theatre clubs, it places greater emphasis on expanding artistic boundaries and exploring the possibilities between theatre, visual art, installation, and new media.

Productions in CCTF 2026

Stone, Sun, Red | 《石头，太阳，红色》 · May 16–17 arrow_forward
